industries.name: Automotive Catalysis & FCC
uses: Cerium oxide (CeO₂, ceria) as oxygen storage component (OSC) in three-way automotive catalysts — ceria reversibly stores and releases oxygen under lean and rich exhaust conditions, maintaining the near-stoichiometric air-fuel ratio needed for simultaneous CO, HC, and NOₓ conversion

Ceria-zirconia mixed oxides (CeO₂-ZrO₂) with superior thermal stability and oxygen storage capacity are the standard OSC material in all modern TWC catalysts — every gasoline vehicle manufactured since the 1990s contains ceria in its catalytic converter

Diesel oxidation catalysts (DOC) use cerium to promote soot combustion and HC/CO oxidation — ceria's redox activity makes it active for soot combustion at lower temperatures than precious metals alone
FCC catalysts for petroleum refining use cerium alongside lanthanum to stabilize zeolite Y and promote vanadium passivation — vanadium poisons FCC catalysts and cerium acts as a vanadium trap

Cerium oxide nanoparticles as fuel-borne catalyst additive — added to diesel fuel to promote combustion of soot particles in diesel particulate filters (DPF), enabling lower-temperature DPF regeneration

companies.name: Johnson Matthey
companies.use: World's largest automotive catalyst supplier. Major consumer of cerium-zirconia mixed oxide for TWC oxygen storage components. Supplies TWC and DOC catalysts containing ceria-zirconia to virtually every major automotive OEM.

companies.name: Umicore
companies.use: Major automotive catalyst manufacturer. Produces ceria-zirconia mixed oxide washcoat materials and complete TWC catalyst systems for gasoline and hybrid vehicles. Also a major rare earth refiner processing cerium from separation plants.

companies.name: Solvay (formerly Rhodia)
companies.use: World's largest producer of ceria-zirconia mixed oxide for automotive catalysis. Supplies OSC powder to catalyst manufacturers including Johnson Matthey, BASF, and Umicore for incorporation into TWC washcoat formulations.

industries.name: Glass Polishing & CMP
uses: Cerium oxide (CeO₂) polishing powder is the world's dominant glass polishing abrasive — used to polish optical lenses, telescope mirrors, flat glass, CRT screens, LCD glass substrates, and architectural glass. CeO₂ polishes glass by a combined mechanical and chemical mechanism faster and with fewer scratches than traditional rouge (Fe₂O₃)

Chemical mechanical planarization (CMP) slurries for semiconductor wafer polishing use cerium oxide particles to planarize silicon dioxide, shallow trench isolation (STI) oxide, and interlayer dielectrics in chip manufacturing
Ophthalmic lens polishing: ceria powder slurry polishes prescription eyeglass lenses to optical quality — the dominant process for high-volume lens manufacturing globally
Solar panel glass and touch screen cover glass polishing uses ceria to achieve the surface smoothness required for optical clarity and touchscreen sensitivity

companies.name: Ferro Corporation (Prince International)
companies.use: Major cerium oxide polishing compound producer. Supplies CeO₂ glass polishing powders to optical, flat glass, and automotive glass manufacturers globally. Also produces CMP slurries for semiconductor oxide planarization.

companies.name: Cabot Microelectronics (CMC Materials)
companies.use: World's largest CMP slurry manufacturer. Produces ceria-based slurries for STI and oxide CMP in semiconductor wafer manufacturing at TSMC, Samsung, Intel, and GlobalFoundries.

industries.name: Glass Decolorizing & UV Absorption
uses: Cerium oxide added to glass formulations absorbs UV radiation — used in automotive windshields, photographic filter glass, and UV-protective ophthalmic lenses to block UV-A and UV-B radiation

Cerium neutralizes green tint in glass caused by iron impurities: cerium oxidizes Fe²⁺ (green) to Fe³⁺ (yellow-brown), and the complementary colors partially cancel — more economical than removing iron from glass batch
Rare earth glass colorants: cerium combined with titanium produces yellow glass; cerium in oxidizing conditions produces faint yellow tints used in decorative glass
Radiation-resistant glass for nuclear and space applications: cerium stabilizes glass against radiation-induced browning (solarization) by trapping electrons that would otherwise create color centers

companies.name: AGC Inc.
companies.use: Major flat glass and specialty glass producer consuming cerium oxide for UV-absorbing automotive glass, decolorizing, and specialty optical glass applications.

companies.name: Essilor (EssilorLuxottica)
companies.use: World's largest ophthalmic lens manufacturer. Uses cerium oxide polishing compounds to finish prescription lenses and incorporates cerium into UV-blocking photochromic lens formulations.

industries.name: Steel & Metallurgy
uses: Misch metal (mixed rare earth metal, primarily Ce+La+Pr+Nd) added to steel as a desulfurizer and inclusion shape modifier — rare earth elements form stable sulfides and oxysulfides, converting elongated manganese sulfide stringers to globular inclusions that improve transverse ductility and toughness

Cerium added to aluminum alloys improves high-temperature strength and oxidation resistance — Ce forms Al-Ce intermetallic phases that remain stable at elevated temperatures; used in lightweight automotive and aerospace castings

Cerium in cast iron: rare earth additions modify graphite morphology in cast iron similarly to magnesium in ductile iron — used as a supplement or alternative to magnesium in producing nodular graphite
Cerium oxide as welding rod coating ingredient — improves arc stability and reduces tungsten contamination in TIG welding electrodes (Ce-W electrodes as alternative to thoriated tungsten)

companies.name: Sandvik
companies.use: Produces cerium-tungsten TIG welding electrodes (WC20 — 2% CeO₂) as a non-radioactive alternative to thoriated tungsten electrodes for TIG welding of stainless steel, titanium, and specialty alloys.

companies.name: MP Materials
companies.use: Operates Mountain Pass rare earth mine in California — the only significant US rare earth producer. Produces mixed rare earth carbonate and separated rare earth products including cerium oxide. Working to establish a US domestic rare earth supply chain.
